title: enum members with implicit values should not collide with other enumerators
description: In an enumeration, it is possible to have several enumerators with the same value. This can be useful in many circumstances. But if that happens without an explicit choice from the developer, it can also lead to errors that are hard to diagnose.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: enum color { red = 3, blue, green, yellow = 5 }; // Noncompliant, both green and yellow = 5
compliant:
enum color { red = 3, blue, green, yellow }; // Compliant, different values
enum color { red = 3, blue, green = 5, yellow = 5 }; // Compliant, all identical values are non-implicit
enum color { red = 3, blue, green, yellow = green }; // Compliant, yellow is defined as identical to green

checks.message: Enum members with implicit values should not collide with explicit values. All enum members should have explicit values or all should use implicit values.
